Meaning & Functions.q: Marketing is the process of:
options: Identifying, anticipating, and satisfying customer needs profitably
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Marketing = understanding and meeting customer needs at a profit.

Meaning & Functions.q: Which is a function of marketing?
options: Gathering market information, product planning, pricing, promotion, distribution
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Marketing covers research, product, price, promotion, and place.

Meaning & Functions.q: Assertion: Marketing starts before production. Reason: Understanding customer needs must happen before creating the product.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Research customer needs FIRST → then design product. Not the other way around.

Meaning & Functions.q: Statement-I: Marketing is customer-oriented. Statement-II: The focus is on satisfying customer needs, not just selling.
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Customer satisfaction is the core of modern marketing.

Meaning & Functions.q: Gathering and analysing market information helps in:
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Market research → understand customers → make better decisions.

Meaning & Functions.q: Product planning and development involves:
options: Deciding what to produce based on customer needs
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Design, features, quality — all based on what customers want.

Meaning & Functions.q: Standardisation and grading involves:
options: Setting quality standards and classifying products by quality levels
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Standards ensure consistency. Grading classifies by quality (Grade A, B, C).

Meaning & Functions.q: Customer support services include:
options: After-sales service, handling complaints, maintenance, and credit facilities
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Post-sale support builds loyalty and repeat purchases.

Meaning & Functions.q: Assertion: Marketing is a social process. Reason: It involves exchange between buyer and seller to satisfy mutual needs.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Exchange of value between parties = social process.

Meaning & Functions.q: Statement-I: Storage and warehousing is a marketing function. Statement-II: Products must be stored until they're needed by customers.
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Storage bridges the time gap between production and consumption.

Meaning & Functions.q: Transportation as a marketing function:
options: Moves goods from place of production to place of consumption
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Physical movement of goods to where customers can buy them.

Meaning & Functions.q: A company conducting surveys to understand customer preferences is performing:
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Surveys = market research = understanding customer needs.

Meaning & Functions.q: Assertion: Marketing creates utility. Reason: It creates time, place, and possession utility for products.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Time (storage), place (transport), possession (selling) utilities.

Meaning & Functions.q: Marketing planning involves:
options: Formulating strategies for marketing mix — product, price, place, promotion
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Comprehensive plan covering all 4Ps.

Meaning & Functions.q: Which is NOT a marketing function?
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Recruitment is staffing, not marketing.

Meaning & Functions.q: A company offering free installation and 3-year warranty provides:
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Installation + warranty = post-sale customer support.

Meaning & Functions.q: Statement-I: Marketing is exchange mechanism. Statement-II: Both parties must offer something of value for exchange to occur.
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Exchange requires both parties gaining value — mutual benefit.

Meaning & Functions.q: Branding, labelling, and packaging are part of:
Meaning & Functions.explanation: How the product is identified, described, and presented = marketing decisions.

Meaning & Functions.q: Assertion: Marketing and selling are the same. Reason: Both involve getting products to customers.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Meaning & Functions.explanation: NOT the same. Marketing is broader — starts with customer needs. Selling starts with the product.

Meaning & Functions.q: Physical distribution includes:
options: Order processing, warehousing, inventory management, and transportation
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Getting the physical product to the right place at the right time.

Meaning & Functions.q: A company that focuses on what customers WANT rather than what it CAN produce follows:
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Marketing concept = customer needs drive product decisions.

Meaning & Functions.q: Statement-I: Marketing creates customer loyalty. Statement-II: Satisfied customers return and recommend the product to others.
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Satisfaction → loyalty → repeat purchase + word-of-mouth.

Meaning & Functions.q: Marketing management philosophies include:
options: Production, Product, Selling, Marketing, and Societal Marketing concepts
Meaning & Functions.explanation: 5 philosophies evolved over time — from production-focus to society-focus.

Meaning & Functions.q: The production concept focuses on:
options: Mass production and wide availability at low cost
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Produce more, make widely available, keep costs low.

Meaning & Functions.q: The product concept focuses on:
options: Continuous improvement in product quality and features
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Better product = more sales. Quality and features drive improvement.

Meaning & Functions.q: The selling concept focuses on:
options: Aggressive selling and promotion to push existing products
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Push the product through persuasion — whether customer needs it or not.

Meaning & Functions.q: The marketing concept focuses on:
options: Customer needs and satisfaction as the path to profit
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Understand needs → create value → satisfy customers → earn profit.

Meaning & Functions.q: The societal marketing concept adds:
options: Consideration of society's long-term well-being alongside customer satisfaction
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Customer satisfaction + societal welfare = societal marketing concept.

Meaning & Functions.q: Assertion: Marketing concept replaced selling concept. Reason: Companies realised satisfying customers is more profitable than pushing products.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Customer-centric approach proved more sustainable than aggressive selling.

Meaning & Functions.q: A tobacco company that satisfies customers but harms society should adopt:
Meaning & Functions.explanation: Must consider social welfare — not just customer wants.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: The 4Ps of marketing mix are:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Product, Price, Place (distribution), Promotion — the 4Ps.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Product in marketing mix refers to:
options: Goods, services, or ideas offered to satisfy customer needs
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Product = everything the customer buys — tangible and intangible benefits.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Price in marketing mix refers to:
options: The amount customers pay — influenced by cost, demand, competition
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Price = what customer pays. Determined by multiple factors.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Assertion: Place refers to distribution — making products available to customers. Reason: Even the best product fails if customers can't access it.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Availability is critical. Great product + unavailable = failure.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Statement-I: Promotion communicates product benefits to target customers. Statement-II: It includes advertising, personal selling, sales promotion, and PR.
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Promotion = all communication activities to inform and persuade.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: A company launches a new smartphone (product), prices it at ₹15,000 (price), sells through Amazon and retail stores (place), and runs TV ads (promotion). This is:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: All four elements working together = marketing mix.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Branding gives a product:
options: A unique identity distinguishing it from competitors
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Brand = name, symbol, design that identifies and differentiates.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Labelling provides:
options: Product information — ingredients, usage, MRP, expiry, manufacturer
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Label = information carrier on the product/package.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Packaging serves:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Packaging = protect + identify + promote + make convenient.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Assertion: All 4Ps must work together. Reason: A premium product priced low or a cheap product in luxury stores creates inconsistency.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: All Ps must be aligned for a consistent marketing strategy.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Factors affecting price include:
options: Cost, demand, competition, government regulation, and marketing objectives
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Multiple factors determine the right price.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Zero-level channel means:
options: Manufacturer sells directly to consumer — no intermediaries
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Direct selling: manufacturer → consumer. No middlemen.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: One-level channel includes:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Manufacturer → Retailer → Consumer. One intermediary.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Two-level channel includes:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Manufacturer → Wholesaler → Retailer → Consumer.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Statement-I: Advertising is paid, non-personal communication. Statement-II: The sponsor bears the cost and is identified.
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Paid (costs money), non-personal (mass media), identified sponsor = advertising.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Personal selling involves:
options: Face-to-face oral presentation to one or more buyers
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Direct personal interaction between salesperson and customer.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Sales promotion includes:
options: Short-term incentives — discounts, free samples, contests, coupons
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Temporary incentives to encourage immediate purchase.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Public relations involves:
options: Building good relations with public through favourable publicity
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: PR = managing company's image and reputation through media, events, CSR.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: A company giving 'Buy 1 Get 1 Free' offer is using:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Short-term incentive to boost immediate sales = sales promotion.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Assertion: The marketing mix must be tailored to the target market. Reason: Different customer segments need different combinations of 4Ps.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Luxury segment vs budget segment need completely different marketing mixes.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Cost-based pricing means:
options: Setting price by adding margin to the total cost of production
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Cost + desired profit margin = selling price.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: A perfume ad in a magazine is:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Magazine ad = paid, non-personal, identified sponsor = advertising.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Statement-I: Product is the most important P. Statement-II: Without a good product, no amount of pricing, placing, or promoting will succeed.
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Product is foundational — other Ps support it.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: A company selling through its own website is using:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Own website = no intermediaries = direct/zero-level channel.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Competition-based pricing means:
options: Setting price based on what competitors charge
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Pricing in relation to competitors — match, undercut, or premium.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: A salesperson visiting a customer's home to demonstrate a vacuum cleaner is:
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Face-to-face product demonstration = personal selling.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Assertion: Promotion is not just advertising. Reason: It includes personal selling, sales promotion, and public relations too.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Promotion = advertising + personal selling + sales promotion + PR.

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Primary packaging is:
options: The first layer of packaging in direct contact with the product
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Primary = directly touches the product (e.g., toothpaste tube).

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Secondary packaging provides:
options: Additional protection and information beyond primary packaging
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Secondary = additional layer (e.g., the box containing the toothpaste tube).

Marketing Mix - 4Ps.q: Statement-I: Marketing mix decisions are interrelated. Statement-II: Changing one P often requires adjusting others.
Marketing Mix - 4Ps.explanation: Raising price may require better promotion. New product may need new channels.

Promotion Elements.q: The most commonly used tool of promotion is:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Advertising is the most widely used and visible promotional tool.

Promotion Elements.q: Advertising is:
options: Paid, non-personal form of communication by an identified sponsor
Promotion Elements.explanation: Paid + non-personal + identified sponsor = advertising.

Promotion Elements.q: Assertion: Personal selling is the most effective for complex products. Reason: The salesperson can explain features, answer questions, and demonstrate.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Promotion Elements.explanation: Complex products need personal explanation — mass media can't do this.

Promotion Elements.q: Statement-I: Sales promotion is short-term. Statement-II: It's designed to encourage immediate purchase, not long-term loyalty.
Promotion Elements.explanation: Sales promotion = temporary incentives for quick action.

Promotion Elements.q: Free samples distributed at a store are:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Free samples = short-term incentive to try the product = sales promotion.

Promotion Elements.q: A company sponsoring a marathon for charity is using:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Building goodwill through community events = public relations.

Promotion Elements.q: Advantages of advertising include:
options: Wide reach, repetition, and cost-effective per person reached
Promotion Elements.explanation: Mass media reaches millions at low cost per exposure.

Promotion Elements.q: A disadvantage of advertising is:
options: One-way communication — no immediate feedback from audience
Promotion Elements.explanation: Ads broadcast messages but can't have conversations with customers.

Promotion Elements.q: Assertion: Personal selling allows immediate feedback. Reason: The salesperson can gauge customer reactions and adjust the pitch.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Promotion Elements.explanation: Two-way interaction → real-time feedback → customised presentation.

Promotion Elements.q: '50% off for today only!' is an example of:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Time-limited discount = urgency-based sales promotion.

Promotion Elements.q: Statement-I: Public relations is free publicity. Statement-II: PR involves managing the company's image through media coverage, events, and community relations.
Promotion Elements.explanation: PR isn't entirely free (events cost money) but earned media coverage IS unpaid. Image management is correct.

Promotion Elements.q: A car company inviting journalists for a test drive event is using:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Media event to generate positive coverage = PR activity.

Promotion Elements.q: Personal selling is most suitable for:
options: High-value, complex products sold to fewer customers
Promotion Elements.explanation: Expensive/complex products (real estate, insurance, B2B) benefit from personal selling.

Promotion Elements.q: A company running a contest 'Win a Trip to Goa' with every purchase is using:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Contest/lucky draw = sales promotion technique.

Promotion Elements.q: Assertion: An effective promotion strategy uses a mix of all four tools. Reason: Different tools reach different audiences in different ways.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Promotion Elements.explanation: Integrated promotion mix = best results. Each tool has unique strengths.

Promotion Elements.q: Publicity is different from advertising because:
options: Publicity is unpaid media coverage; advertising is paid
Promotion Elements.explanation: Publicity = earned/unpaid coverage. Advertising = paid placement.

Promotion Elements.q: A company's press release about a new product launch is:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Press release to media = PR activity generating publicity.

Promotion Elements.q: Statement-I: Sales promotion tools include coupons, discounts, free gifts, and loyalty programmes. Statement-II: These provide short-term incentives to boost sales.
Promotion Elements.explanation: All are sales promotion techniques designed for immediate purchase motivation.

Promotion Elements.q: The role of promotion in marketing mix is to:
options: Inform, persuade, and remind target customers about the product
Promotion Elements.explanation: Inform → Persuade → Remind. Three roles of promotion.

Promotion Elements.q: A salesperson who builds a long-term relationship with a business client is using:
Promotion Elements.explanation: B2B relationship building through personal interaction = personal selling.

Promotion Elements.q: Assertion: Digital marketing is a form of advertising. Reason: Online ads on Google, Facebook, and Instagram are paid, non-personal, and sponsor-identified.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Promotion Elements.explanation: Digital ads meet all criteria of advertising — just a different medium.

Promotion Elements.q: A company donating to flood relief and announcing it in the media is using:
Promotion Elements.explanation: CSR + media coverage = PR building company reputation.

Promotion Elements.q: Which promotion tool is MOST expensive per customer reached?
Promotion Elements.explanation: One salesperson per customer = high per-person cost. But effective for complex sales.

Promotion Elements.q: Which promotion tool is LEAST expensive per customer reached?
Promotion Elements.explanation: One ad reaches millions = very low cost per person exposed.

Promotion Elements.q: Statement-I: All promotion elements work together in an integrated promotion mix. Statement-II: The right mix depends on product type, target market, and budget.
Promotion Elements.explanation: Integration + customisation = effective promotion strategy.

Promotion Elements.q: A loyalty card programme (buy 10, get 1 free) is:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Reward for repeat purchase = sales promotion technique.

Promotion Elements.q: Trade promotion (discounts to wholesalers/retailers) is aimed at:
Promotion Elements.explanation: Trade promotion motivates distributors/retailers to stock and push the product.

Promotion Elements.q: Assertion: Advertising creates awareness; personal selling closes the sale. Reason: Different tools play different roles in the buying process.
options: Both correct, Reason does not explain
Promotion Elements.explanation: Advertising → awareness. Personal selling → conversion. Complementary roles.

Promotion Elements.q: An influencer promoting a product on Instagram is closest to:
options: Mix of advertising and personal selling
Promotion Elements.explanation: Paid (like ad) but personal recommendation (like selling) = hybrid.

Promotion Elements.q: The promotion tool that offers the MOST credibility is:
Promotion Elements.explanation: News coverage and third-party endorsement = more trusted than paid ads.
